etymology.description: 陰(陰) (yīn, "cloudy; negative; hidden") indicates cloudy or shadowed conditions, as it typically represents the shaded or obscure side of a hill, metaphorically linked to overcast weather; 天(天) (tiān, "sky; heaven; day") is the sky or celestial space, relating to the weather above us.
etymology.interpretation: 陰(陰) (yīn, "cloudy; hidden") conveys shadowy or overcast weather, implying the presence of clouds that obscure sunlight; it also connotes negativity as clouds can bring gloom. 天(天) (tiān, "sky") naturally represents the weather or atmosphere, so 阴天(陰天) (yīntiān) literally combines these to signify 'cloudy sky' or 'overcast weather', as a sky shadowed by clouds blocking the sun's light.

examples.chinese: 今天是阴天(今天是陰天)。
阴天的时候(陰天的時候)，我喜欢喝热茶(我喜歡喝熱茶)。
examples.pinyin: Yīntiān de shíhòu, wǒ xǐhuān hē rè chá.
examples.english: When it's cloudy, I like to drink hot tea.

examples.chinese: 阴天让人感觉很放松(陰天讓人感覺很放鬆)。
examples.pinyin: Yīntiān ràng rén gǎnjué hěn fàngsōng.
examples.english: Cloudy weather makes people feel relaxed.
